title: Citrusy Dense Poached Pineapple Stir-fry
description: A vibrant and delightful citrusy, dense pineapple dish infused with vanilla extract, perfect for a crowd-pleasing Canadian meal that is also pescatarian-friendly.

ingredients.name: 1 cup sliced fresh pineapples
ingredients.notes: Choose a sweet variety for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Juice of 3 lemons
ingredients.notes: To infuse the pineapples with tangy citrus.

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup freshly squeezed lime juice
ingredients.notes: For an extra burst of freshness and acidity.

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons honey or maple syrup
ingredients.notes: To enhance the natural sweetness and add a touch of depth.

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup cornstarch
ingredients.notes: Helps thicken the sauce and makes it richer and more complex.

ingredients.name: 3 cups water or vegetable stock
ingredients.notes: Used to poach the pineapple slices in a flavorful broth.

ingredients.name: 1/2 cup milk (dairy or dairy-free)
ingredients.notes: Complements the sweetness and enhances the overall texture of the dish.

ingredients.name: Juice of 1 lemon
ingredients.notes: Used as a natural preservative to prevent browning, ensuring vibrant colors.

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons vanilla extract
ingredients.notes: Intensifies the fruit's sweetness and adds a luxurious touch.

instructions: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Rinse the pineapples under cold water, then pat dry with a clean kitchen towel.
Mix the granulated sugar, honey or maple syrup, freshly squeezed lime juice, and cornstarch in a small saucepan. Bring it to a boil over medium-high heat until thickened, stirring occasionally.

In a large bowl, combine the poaching liquid (3 cups water or vegetable stock), cinnamon, vanilla extract, fresh lemon juice, and honey or maple syrup from step 2. Slice the pineapple into chunks and add them to the bowl. Toss gently so that all slices are coated evenly with the mixture.
Pour half of the poaching solution into a large baking dish, nestle the coated pineapples in it, then cover with foil. Bake for about 30 minutes or until tender but still firm.

In another pot, heat the remaining poaching liquid and stir in the thickened cornstarch mixture from step 2. Stir continuously to avoid lumps, reduce the heat to low, and let the sauce simmer until it becomes a rich glaze (about 15 minutes).

To serve, gently pour some of the thickened poaching liquid over each pineapple slice. Top with a dollop of dairy-free or dairy-based yogurt if desired. Garnish with fresh mint leaves for added freshness.
Let the stir-fry cool slightly before serving to avoid spilling hot liquids.
